meta.title: TechPulse
meta.tagline: Tech & AI news, hot takes, and who's saying what — from the builders you follow
meta.note: Built from your X Following timeline, filtered to tech/AI builders by profile. Click any story to see every take and what different people are saying.

stories.title: The Fable vs GPT-5.6 Sol Rivalry Rages On
stories.summary: The Claude-vs-OpenAI flagship debate is still the main event: some builders swear by Fable's quality, others have fully switched to GPT-5.6 Sol, and OpenAI's Sam Altman keeps taking not-so-subtle jabs at the competition while Grok 4.5's scale keeps climbing.

takes.name: Theo - t3.gg
takes.text: I still prefer Fable btw

takes.name: James Perkins
takes.text: Fable still on top for me

takes.name: Micky
takes.text: Fable > 5.6 Sol

takes.name: Ben Davis
takes.text: I still prefer GPT-5.6 btw

takes.name: Layton Gott
takes.text: I can only imagine what GPT 5.6 Sol Ultra is like...

takes.name: Alem Tuzlak 🇧🇦
takes.text:
@sama The only thing that bugs me about the new models is the fact that they are very slow. I've been running sol on fast mode qnd it took it 4 hours to review and write an implementation doc. 

I stopped it and told it to wrap it up and spend the remainder of my day using it to

takes.name: Sam Altman
takes.text: come for the best model, stay because we don’t treat you with contempt

takes.name: Mirko Monti
takes.text:
So, Grok 4.5 is a 1.5 T model, Grok 5 would be 10T model... then Grok 6 will be a 100T models? 

Is there a limit?

stories.title: Agent Skills Tooling Keeps Compounding
stories.summary: The 'skills' ecosystem for coding agents keeps growing fast: Kent C. Dodds is building out community integration packages for his Kody agent, Matt Pocock is livestreaming a new agent tool called Wayfinder, and Emil Kowalski's skills repo just crossed 8,000 GitHub stars in a week.

takes.name: Emil Kowalski
takes.text:
My skills repo has gained 8,000 GitHub stars in a week, it surpassed Vaul and is 300 stars short of surpassing Sonner. 

Not greatest metrics of success, but it’s a good indication of how the times change. https://t.co/tpdjhy92Ly

takes.name: Kent C. Dodds 🏹
takes.text: One nice thing about the way @kodykoala works is that it doesn't come with built-in integrations hosted by Kody. Instead Kody helps you create the integration yourself giving you complete control over what Kody can do with your integration. https://t.co/b0kIUXxJNT

takes.name: Kent C. Dodds 🏹
takes.text:
I just published a @raycast community package for @kodykoala https://t.co/mkr0aD4CR3

I use this to bridge into my own raycast extension which allows me to have quick shortcuts to trigger whatever I want with Kody. Right now I'm using it to open and close my shades ☀️

takes.name: Kent C. Dodds 🏹
takes.text: Yo @resend, you got a package in @kodykoala https://t.co/bDXn5CNFfA 🔥

takes.name: joel ⛈️
takes.text: published skills are often best used as inspiration https://t.co/d9164xnLKJ

takes.name: Matt Pocock
takes.text:
Going live to demo /wayfinder

Time to get your questions in now!

https://t.co/pqd1tduyQP

stories.title: Vercel Keeps Shipping Agent-Native Dev Tooling
stories.summary: Vercel had a busy stretch: CLI-managed feature flag targeting rules (now framed as a building block for autonomous, self-optimizing agents), a 10% cost cut on Web Analytics, subagent visibility in Agent Runs, and early TypeScript 7 support landing in Next.js.

takes.name: Vercel Developers
takes.text:
You can now manage targeting rules for Vercel Flags with Vercel CLI.

𝚟𝚌 𝚏𝚕𝚊𝚐𝚜 𝚛𝚞𝚕𝚎𝚜 𝚊𝚍𝚍 𝚗𝚎𝚠-𝚌𝚑𝚎𝚌𝚔𝚘𝚞𝚝 \
  --𝚌𝚘𝚗𝚍𝚒𝚝𝚒𝚘𝚗 "𝚞𝚜𝚎𝚛.𝚌𝚘𝚞𝚗𝚝𝚛𝚢:𝚒𝚗:𝙳𝙴,𝙴𝚂" \
  --𝚟𝚊𝚛𝚒𝚊𝚗𝚝 𝚗𝚎𝚠-𝚌𝚑𝚎𝚌𝚔𝚘𝚞𝚝
https://t.co/c2s0teHUoq

takes.name: Vercel Developers
takes.text:
We shipped a 10% improvement in cost-efficiency for Web Analytics and Speed Insights.

Pro & Enterprise customers will see the savings reflected in their next bill.

https://t.co/8TMwXXNARJ

takes.name: Vercel Developers
takes.text:
Agent Runs now shows subagent activity for @evedev_ projects.

Inspect each run in full, including turns, model and tool calls, cost, and token usage.

https://t.co/sha40IgCrT

takes.name: Guillermo Rauch
takes.text:
Powerful building block for autonomous, self-optimizing websites and applications.

Give agents the ability to set up and tune experiments with feature flags.

takes.name: Guillermo Rauch
takes.text:
The two most popular https://t.co/99eEa13mZ3 features so far have been:

1️⃣ The ease of use / filesystem API
2️⃣ Observability 

We’re doubling down on both!

takes.name: Tim
takes.text:
TypeScript 7 Support in Next.js

TLDR: 
You can now enable `experimental.useTypeScriptCli` to use TypeScript 7 on next@preview

Details:

TypeScript 7 released with ts-go, very excited about the speedup!

The release is (intentionally) missing the JavaScript API which they're https://t.co/Ly25fB61NN

stories.title: Coding Agents Are Getting Full Browser Access
stories.summary: Agent-to-browser integration is having a moment: Browserbase's CEO showcased how Julius went from just answering questions to taking real browser actions, while Codex and Claude Code both shipped in-app browsers with multi-tab support and cookie/password import within days of each other.

takes.name: Browserbase
Paul Klein IV
takes.text:
every great agent needs a browser. 

we’re excited to continue to power great companies building great agents.

congrats to the Julius team on the big launch!

takes.name: Riley Brown
takes.text:
Codex just updated their in app browser... You can now open multiple tabs, import cookies and passwords and more. 

And then 2 days later Claude Code released a similar update...

This completely shifts the way we use computers...
Here's everything you need to know.

00:00 Intro https://t.co/5SJx7aAtsC

stories.title: Opencode Leans Into Community-Driven Development
stories.summary: The opencode team is routing GitHub issues straight into the agent for reproduction and fixes, building out a Slack bot integration, and doubling down on making the open-source coding agent option genuinely high quality, not just free.

takes.name: dax
takes.text:
we have an issues channel where every good github issue goes and we can ask opencode to reproduce it and fix

even started throwing posts from here in there https://t.co/r8NqTJYAW9

takes.name: dax
takes.text:
so this ended up going super well and people had a really fun time with it

we're gonna drop it publicly tomorrow on the @anomalyco account

takes.name: vimtor
takes.text: big awakenings happening with the opencode slack bot https://t.co/HbR7Gkhp9o

takes.name: dax
takes.text:
the opensource option tends to be shittier but you use it because it's free

we're trying to make the opensource option also the high quality option

stories.title: Do You Miss Programming By Hand?
stories.summary: As AI agents take over more of the actual typing, a few builders are getting wistful about hand-coding, ThePrimeagen mourns losing the fun of the process itself while Rasmic admits he misses it too but isn't turning back.

takes.name: ThePrimeagen
takes.text:
This actually makes me sad.  Programming is such a fun thing to do in of itself.  The completion of the thing task is the ultimate goal, but the road to get there is enjoyable!

Even as i investigate loops and think deeply about how to deploy AI in a real manner that produces

takes.name: Micky
takes.text:
Listen, i know a lot of dudes that love and miss programming by hand (me)

But at the end of the day, we tryna make it out of the trenches and gotta get with the times

plus using AI can very very be fun

stories.title: Theo's T3 Code Keeps Evolving: New Sidebar, Codex Ultra Deep Dive
stories.summary: Theo is running multiple dev boxes entirely through T3 Code now and thinks his own sidebar UX is starting to feel dated at this level of agent productivity, while also breaking down what Codex's new 'Ultra' mode actually does.

takes.name: Theo - t3.gg
takes.text:
It's still so, so cool that I can control all of my dev boxes through T3 Code. I barely open the desktop app anymore, I just use the site and the app.

Working on 3 projects with 2 harnesses across 4 dev machines, all from one (open source) interface 🫡 https://t.co/fveG2WyfQ2

takes.name: Theo - t3.gg
takes.text:
I will admit that the level of productivity possible is making a lot of the UX feel less than ideal. I'm at the point where I think the "sidebar grouped by projects" barely makes sense anymore.

Going to play around with some new concepts to see how we can go even further

takes.name: Theo - t3.gg
takes.text: "Ultra" mode is not what you think. I did a deep dive on Codex's newest feature to try and help y'all understand it better. https://t.co/bAqLBDLZVq

takes.name: Theo - t3.gg
takes.text: I want to try a new experimental sidebar for threads in T3 Code. Generated a few options. Which do you like best? https://t.co/UIPXHS6dFt

stories.title: SpaceXAI Reverses Course, Deletes All Uploaded User Data
stories.summary: After defending SpaceX's data retention policy as helpful for debugging, Elon Musk did an abrupt U-turn hours later, announcing all user data ever uploaded to SpaceXAI would be completely deleted as a precaution.

takes.name: Elon Musk
takes.text:
SpaceX policy regarding data retention.

It is actually helpful for debugging issues if we can retain some amount of data, so allowing this would be appreciated, but your privacy settings are always respected.

takes.name: Elon Musk
takes.text:
True. 

As a precautionary measure, all user data that was uploaded to SpaceXAI before now will be completely and utterly deleted. Zero anything whatsoever will remain.

stories.title: Fei-Fei Li's Stanford BEHAVIOR Robotics Challenge Returns
stories.summary: Fei-Fei Li kicked off a new round of Stanford's BEHAVIOR Challenge, arguing that long-horizon, real-world robotics tasks (planning, manipulation, failure recovery) remain unsolved even as today's models get better at everything else.

takes.name: Fei-Fei Li
takes.text:
1/N Long horizon, complex tasks that truly matter in everyday life are not solved problems by today’s robotics, requiring planning, object detection, object manipulation, and failure recovery.

That's why Stanford's BEHAVIOR Challenge is back for year 2! Last year, the winning https://t.co/4mSPwzKWJM

stories.title: Kaku Ships a Faster AI-Native Terminal
stories.summary: HiTw93 pushed two new releases of Kaku, a terminal built specifically for AI coding workflows, adding faster startup, jump-to-any-pane in split tabs, and more reliable session restore.

takes.name: Tw93
takes.text:
🎃 Kaku is a fast, out-of-the-box terminal built for AI coding.

Two new releases: faster startup, prompts, and AI chat, plus jump-to-any-pane in split tabs, cleaner renaming and selection, and more reliable session restore.
https://t.co/cJVSaw22Ux https://t.co/R7P49vuaFp

hotTakes.name: shadcn
hotTakes.text: I like how GPTs talk to me. OpenAI got this right.

hotTakes.name: Sam Altman
hotTakes.text: i thought this was satire, kept looking for the handle to be spelled c1audeai or something

hotTakes.name: vaxry
hotTakes.text:
So, will we get something that is absolutely not private and can be tracked, or will we get something trivially bypassable by spoofing the response?

I'm taking bets!!!!!!

hotTakes.name: hayden
hotTakes.text: loveable and netlify have done irreparable damage to CEOs everywhere

hotTakes.name: Ridd 🤿
hotTakes.text:
the UX of Hey is truly an abomination

but man as soon as I try other clients I am 10x more overwhelmed because everything is so organized in Hey that I start to freak out

hotTakes.name: cami
hotTakes.text:
Ok after using codex for a couple weeks it’s pretty clear I have to cancel my $200 anthropic subscription

My new ai stack is now
Codex - coding & computer use  
Poncho - lead generation & social media growth hacking
Midjourney - best image gen imo 
Higgsfield - all in one for my

hotTakes.name: Mike Futia
hotTakes.text:
I just built a Meta ad policy checker in Claude Code that catches rejections BEFORE Meta does 🤯

Drop in your ad copy → it pulls Meta's LIVE Advertising Standards, checks every line against the actual policy text, and hands each ad a verdict: Cleared for launch, Fix before https://t.co/TVVl6QxTqN
